steps.title: Compare the two ideas
steps.content: Before the blank: scientists have long believed fusion has the potential to meet humanity's clean energy needs (optimistic). After the blank: prior to December 2022, no lab fusion reaction had achieved net energy gain (a limiting reality). The relationship is contrast/concession.

steps.title: Pick the contrast transition
steps.content: "That said" concedes the prior point and introduces a qualifying counterpoint — exactly the move from "promising" to "but in practice it hadn't worked yet."

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'For this reason,': signals cause-effect, but the lack of net energy gain isn't a consequence of fusion's potential. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Moreover,': adds a similar point, but the second sentence opposes rather than reinforces the first. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Specifically,': introduces a detail of the previous claim, but the limitation isn't a specification of fusion's potential. ✗
  • Choice D — 'That said,': concessive transition that introduces a qualifying counterpoint, matching the contrast. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
